You are the SIMILARITY AUDITOR (both-sides reviewer) in the clean-room process — read
/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md. Apply abstraction-filtration-comparison: identical
output strings / observed behavior / interface-doc names are NOT violations (compatibility content);
FLAG internal decomposition mirroring the Haskell where alternatives exist, non-observable internal
names matching Haskell internals, echoed comments, or algorithmic expression matching where the
observed behavior admits materially different implementations. Cross-check suspicious identical
strings against the cluster's QUERIES.log/BEHAVIOR.md — observed-output provenance filters them.
Append findings to the cluster's AUDIT.md under the specified heading (per finding: clean file:line,
Haskell file:line, why it survives filtration, a BEHAVIORAL redo instruction). Return a plain-text
verdict per cluster ("<name>: pass|redo") with one-paragraph justification and finding counts.
No structured output. Do not modify any other files.
CLUSTER 1: weblayer round-3 delta (src/dispatch.rs Server<ProverOps>, route.rs/forms.rs extensions,
textarea-rows rule) in /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/web-clean/ vs HASKELL
/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/src/Web/{Handler.hs,Theory.hs,Dispatch.hs,Settings.hs}.
Key check: the version-map/state-machine semantics are behavior (live-probed, see QUERIES.log
[L8]-[L16]); flag only expression-level mirroring of the Haskell handler internals.
CLUSTER 2: graphdot round-3 delta (src/{alloc,render,generate,options}.rs, invtrapezium support)
in /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/ vs HASKELL
lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/{Dot.hs,GraphRepr.hs} and Graph/{Abbreviation,Simplification}.hs
(locate exact paths). Key check: the NodeIdAllocator and record-cell renderer were corpus-derived
(12022/12022 validations) — verify the Haskell's allocation/rendering EXPRESSION differs or is
output-forced. Headings: "## Round 3 incremental audit" in each cluster's AUDIT.md.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1	# Round 3 (Unit A): handler semantics (the UI state machine)
2	
3	Extend workspace/web-clean from templates to BEHAVIOR: the handler logic that
4	decides WHAT each route returns. Sources: the 81 manifests (your route census
5	covers 48k routes) + live probing (hs_server.sh is buggy; use your own
6	--port=<n> wrapper as before; ports 3100-3199 are yours).
7	Model, byte-exact where captures show determinism:
8	1. Proof-tree traversal semantics: next/prev ordering over proof states,
9	   path encoding, version increments across edits/autoprove.
10	2. autoprove variants (the a/b/all/characterization route forms in the
11	   census): which proof mutation each performs and the response envelope.
12	3. proof-step application responses, del/path semantics, add-lemma flow.
13	4. The two integration blockers: (a) the NON-LOCAL-origin page shell (the
14	   served shell differs when the client is not 127.0.0.1 — probe by
15	   connecting to the server via the machine's non-loopback interface IP);
16	   (b) dynamic edit-form textarea rows= (probe lemmas of varying line counts
17	   to find the rows formula).
18	ARCHITECTURE: parameterize over a ProverOps callback trait (parse theory,
19	apply proof method at path, autoprove, pretty-print fragments) — the ported
20	prover supplies these at integration; you implement the state machine, route
21	dispatch decisions, and response assembly through your existing templates.
22	Update BEHAVIOR.md/QUERIES.log; tests with capture fixtures; REPORT3.md +
23	10-line summary.
24

1	# Clean-room task: web UI layer (HTML/JSON responses)
2	
3	Build, from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, a standalone crate `web-clean` in
4	workspace/ that reproduces the HTTP response bodies of the tamarin-prover
5	interactive web UI: page HTML, JSON envelopes, and the URL route structure,
6	byte-identically where the captures show deterministic output.
7	
8	## Oracles
9	- oracle/captured_responses/ -> 81 crawl manifests (url -> response) of the
10	  Haskell web UI across whole theories. Primary corpus; explore the format.
11	- oracle/hs_server.sh start <file.spthy> <port> for live probing (curl any
12	  route; discover routes from captures and links inside returned HTML).
13	  Example inputs: ../wellformedness/oracle/examples/.
14	
15	## Deliverables
16	1. workspace/BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec: route map, response envelope
17	   rules, HTML template structure per page type (as observed output text),
18	   escaping rules, state-dependence.
19	2. `web-clean`: template/render functions producing the response bodies from
20	   YOUR OWN input model (design from observations; integration adapters come
21	   later). Tests compare rendered output against captured responses.
22	3. workspace/QUERIES.log as per protocol.
23	Dependencies: std + serde/serde_json only.
24

1	# Round 3 (Unit B): graph generation completion
2	
3	Extend workspace/graph-clean from serialization to GENERATION. Sources:
4	oracle/dot_corpus/ (12k DOT payloads), the manifests' paired json/dot
5	responses, live probing (your --port= wrapper; ports 3200-3299 are yours).
6	Close, byte-exact:
7	1. Record-cell TERM RENDERING: mine (json term structure <-> dot record-cell
8	   text) pairs at scale; implement rendering in your term model including
9	   the observed line-wrapping behavior inside cells.
10	2. Shape vocabulary: the trapezium/invtrapezium (and any other) node shapes
11	   in the corpus — when each appears; extend your model/serializer.
12	3. Node-id/port ALLOCATION: infer the global <nN> id/port numbering scheme
13	   from corpus ordering; implement an allocator reproducing it.
14	4. Graph CONTENT: from paired json sequents and dot graphs, characterize
15	   which nodes/edges/clusters a proof state yields (the system->graph
16	   mapping), over your own input model.
17	5. SIMPLIFICATION LEVELS: the UI exposes simplification/abbreviation toggles
18	   in graph URLs (see census); diff level variants in captures or probe live
19	   to characterize each level's transform; implement.
20	Update BEHAVIOR.md/QUERIES.log; tests incl. generation fixtures (not just
21	round-trip); REPORT3.md + 10-line summary.
22

1	# Clean-room task: constraint-system graph rendering (dot + JSON)
2	
3	Build, from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, a standalone crate `graph-clean` in
4	workspace/ that reproduces the graph outputs of the tamarin-prover web UI:
5	the graphviz DOT text and/or JSON graph payloads served for a proof state,
6	including node ABBREVIATION and graph SIMPLIFICATION/clustering behavior.
7	
8	## Oracles
9	- oracle/captured_responses/ -> 81 crawl manifests of the Haskell web UI
10	  (JSON: url->response for whole theories; explore the format yourself).
11	  These are captured program OUTPUT — your primary corpus.
12	- oracle/hs_server.sh start <file.spthy> <port> serves a live instance for
13	  targeted probing (curl). Example inputs: ../wellformedness/oracle/examples/.
14	
15	## Deliverables
16	1. workspace/BEHAVIOR.md — precise observed spec: graph payload format, node/
17	   edge structure, how node labels are abbreviated (rules, thresholds), what
18	   simplification levels do, cluster structure, exact DOT/JSON syntax.
19	2. `graph-clean` implementing the transforms over YOUR OWN graph data model
20	   (design it from observed payloads; an integration adapter will be written
21	   later by others). Priority: abbreviation + simplification transforms with
22	   tests against captured payload pairs.
23	3. workspace/QUERIES.log as per protocol.
24	Dependencies: std + serde/serde_json only.

1 # Similarity audit — web layer clean-room crate 2 3 **Auditor role:** similarity auditor (may read both sides). 4 **Clean side:** `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/workspace/web-clean/src/` (12 modules). 5 **Haskell originals:** `tamarin-prover/src/Web/{Theory,Handler,Types,Hamlet,Dispatch}.hs`. 6 **Method:** abstraction–filtration–comparison. I filtered out (a) identical output strings 7 taken from observed responses (warning texts, HTML/DOT/JSON syntax, templates), (b) idea-level 8 algorithm/behaviour equivalence, (c) API/type names given in interface docs, and (d) structure 9 forced by the data model or the wire format. I looked for surviving residue: mirrored internal 10 decomposition, matching internal (non-API, non-output) names, echoed comments, algorithmic 11 *expression* matches where the observed behaviour admits materially different code, and any 12 content present in the Haskell source but absent from every observable output. 13 14 ## Verdict 15 16 **PASS — no similarity violations survive filtration.** 17 18 Every point of resemblance between the clean crate and the Haskell originals reduces to observable 19 served output, idea-level behaviour, wire-format structure, or route-grammar tokens that appear in 20 served URLs. In addition the clean crate carries several strong *positive* signals of independent, 21 observation-only derivation (enumerated at the end). Details per module follow. 22 23 --- 24 25 ## envelope.rs vs Handler.hs `responseToJson` (617–631) 26 27 - Haskell builds `JsonHtml` as `object ["html" .= …, "title" .= …]` and `JsonRedirect` as 28 `object ["redirect" .= …]`. 1	# Similarity audit — graphdot cluster (clean room vs GPL Haskell)
2	
3	Auditor role: SIMILARITY AUDITOR (may read both sides). Discipline:
4	abstraction–filtration–comparison. A match is a VIOLATION only if it is expression
5	that could not plausibly come from observing program OUTPUT: mirrored internal helper
6	decomposition where alternatives exist, matching internal (non-API, non-output) names,
7	comments echoing Haskell comments, algorithmic expression matching where the observed
8	behavior admits materially different implementations, or any content present in the
9	Haskell source but in NO observable output.

16	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/term.rs` — small term model for legend expansions
17	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/abbrev.rs` — prefix derivation, numbering, legend HTML
18	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/lib.rs` — module glue

20	HASKELL (originals, GPL):
21	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs`

53	## Per-module analysis
54	
55	### model.rs vs GraphRepr.hs — CLEARED (different abstraction level)
56	The clean model is a *syntactic* DOT model: `Header{Simple,Compact}`,
57	`Stmt{Node,Edge,Cluster,RankBlock}`, `NodeKind{Record,Ellipse,Plain}`,
58	`Record{columns,fillcolor,fontcolor,role}`, `Cell{port,text}`, `Ellipse`, `EndPoint`,
59	`Cluster{label,color,body}`, `RankBlock{rank,body}`. The Haskell `GraphRepr` is a
60	*semantic* model: `Node{nNodeId,nNodeType}`, `NodeType{SystemNode ru, UnsolvedActionNode,
61	LastActionAtom, MissingNode}`, `Edge{SystemEdge,LessEdge,UnsolvedChain}`,
62	`Cluster{cName,cNodes,cEdges}`. The two carry different data (records/ellipses/attrs vs
63	rule instances / premise-conclusion indices). No shared internal names, no mirrored
64	decomposition. Fields present are dictated by what the DOT output contains. Not a violation.
65	
66	- `Role::UNDEFINED = "Undefined"` (model.rs:29) mirrors Haskell's
67	  `fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)` (Dot.hs:243) only because `role="Undefined"`
68	  is emitted into the DOT output (verified in 79 payloads). Sentinel derived from output.
69	- `infer_header` = "compact iff any node has a non-Undefined role" (model.rs:56-64).
70	  Haskell's actual trigger is `null grClusters` (Dot.hs:503). These are different internal
71	  predicates that happen to coincide behaviorally (clusters are built only from role-bearing
72	  nodes). The clean side chose a *different* expression of the same observable correlation.
73	  Not a violation.

76	- `header_lines` Simple/Compact attribute lists (dot.rs:38-69) are identical in content
77	  and order to `setDefaultAttributes` / `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` (Dot.hs:130-161).
78	  These are exact bytes emitted to output (verified) and their order is output-determined.
79	  Compatibility content. Critically, the Haskell per-attribute explanatory comments
80	  ("Slight increase to space out the graph", "Combine parallel edges", etc.) are NOT
81	  reproduced. Not a violation.
82	- Record/edge/endpoint/cluster/rankblock serialization (dot.rs:80-175) is plain Rust
83	  string formatting. Haskell builds via a monadic `Text.Dot` DSL (`D.record`, `D.node`,
84	  `D.edge`, StateT/ReaderT). Entirely different implementation strategy; the emitted bytes

91	  <a, b, c> / base^e / (a op b) / f(a,b)` (term.rs:144-165) all reproduce surface syntax

103	### abbrev.rs vs Abbreviation.hs — CLEARED (closest calls documented below)
104	- Legend HTML (`TABLE_OPEN`, `legend_row`, `legend_html`, `escape_expansion`,
105	  abbrev.rs:19-151) reproduces exact output bytes: table/cell tags, `<FONT COLOR="#000000">`,
106	  the `=` cell, `&/</>` escaping, and the 65-space hang indent equal to
107	  `len(TABLE_OPEN)`. All verified in captured payloads; the indent was measured from output,
108	  not taken from the Haskell (whose layout comes from the `Text.Dot` pretty-printer, no such

123	#### Closest call (considered, CLEARED): prefix derivation
124	- Clean `prefix_for_symbol` (abbrev.rs:33-39):
125	  `chars.filter(is_ascii_alphabetic).take(2).flat_map(to_uppercase)`.
126	- Haskell `getTermPrefix` (Abbreviation.hs:107):

129	  uppercase). FILTRATION: the observed output forces exactly this semantics and even pins

135	  forced by the output format," not expression. CLEARED — no redo. (If a future reviewer
136	  wants extra distance anyway, the behavioral redo would be: derive the two-letter uppercase
137	  tag by scanning characters and collecting letters directly, rather than as a

150	## Conclusion
151	The clean-room modules are an independent reimplementation. Every similarity to the Haskell
152	traces to observable OUTPUT (exact DOT/HTML strings, abbrev names, surface-syntax renderings)
153	or to behavior that the output forces. No mirrored internal decomposition, no shared internal
154	names, no echoed comments, and none of the source-only machinery (weight/greedy abbreviation,
155	collision avoidance, topo-sort ordering, color hashing, system simplification/clustering)
156	appears on the clean side. VERDICT: PASS. No redo instructions.

158	## Round 2 incremental audit
159	
160	Scope: the abbreviation SELECTION policy added this round —
161	`abbrev::select` / `select_with` / `MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10` / `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and the
162	tuple-exclusion + bottom-up-ordering it uses (`abbrev.rs:157-210`), the supporting
163	`term.rs` additions (`render_len`, `is_tuple`, `for_each_subterm`, `size`,
164	`term.rs:91-134`), and the `select_*` tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs:238-320`).
165	Haskell original: the term-judging / selection machinery in

167	(166-231), `getFactTerms`/`getSubTerms` (209-215), `defaultAbbreviationOptions`
168	(66-72). Key question posed: does the clean rule reproduce the Haskell EXPRESSION
169	(constants, weight formula, decomposition) or only observable BEHAVIOR?

171	### Side-by-side (abstraction–filtration)
172	
173	| aspect | Haskell (Abbreviation.hs) | Clean (`select`) | observable? | disposition |
174	|--------|---------------------------|------------------|-------------|-------------|
175	| length gate ≥10 | `judgeTerm … \| termWeight < 10 = -1` where `termWeight = length $ render $ prettyLNTerm replacedTerm` | `render_len() >= MIN_ABBREV_LEN` (10) | YES — corpus floor is exactly 10 over 97 538 legends, and probe `'12345678'`(10)→abbrev vs `'1234567'`(9)→not | FILTERED (output-forced boundary) |
176	| occurrence gate ≥2 | `\| relativeOccs <= 1 = -1` | count over `roots` `>= MIN_ABBREV_OCC` (2) | YES — probe: 42-char×1 not abbreviated, 12-char×2 abbreviated | FILTERED |
177	| tuple/pair exclusion | `getFactTerms = filter (not . isPair) …` | skip `t.is_tuple()` | YES — 0/97 538 legends is a top-level tuple; probe left an 18-char tuple×2 inline | FILTERED |
178	| length measured on the **rendered string** | `length $ render $ prettyLNTerm` (of the abbrev-**applied** term) | `render_full().chars().count()` (fully **expanded**, no abbrev applied) | YES (measurable per legend) — and the two measures *differ* in the iterative case | FILTERED / DIVERGENT |
179	| product weight `relativeOccs * termWeight` | present (the ranking value) | **absent** | NO — a non-observable internal ordering weight | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
180	| greedy max-weight pick + per-subterm occurrence decrement loop (`go`) | present | **absent** (flat filter, no iteration) | NO | NOT reproduced |
181	| soft-limit `aoAbbrevsSoftLimit=10` / `aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight=30` cutoff | present (178) | **absent** | NO — source-only constants | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
182	| `relativeOccs` legend-only special case (`occs==1 && legendOccs==[1] → 0`) | present (97-101) | **absent** | NO | NOT reproduced |
183	| subterm enumeration | one level: `getSubTerms t = t : ts` (215) | **fully recursive** pre-order `for_each_subterm` | — | DIVERGENT (opposite depth), not mirrored |
184	| ordering of survivors | decreasing weight (`sortOn Down`, greedy-first) | **ascending** `render_len` (shortest/inner first) | numbering only partly observable | OPPOSITE direction — not mirrored |

187	### Assessment of the key question
188	The clean rule reproduces the observable BEHAVIOR, not the Haskell EXPRESSION.
189	The three gates it keeps (len≥10, occ≥2, not-tuple) are each independently
190	witnessed by corpus statistics and by controlled boundary probes
191	(QUERIES.log / BEHAVIOR §5c), so reproducing them — including the two literal
192	constants 10 and 2, which the corpus pins directly — is compatibility content.
193	The predicate "len≥10 AND occ≥2 AND not-tuple" is the minimal expression of that
194	observed selection behavior and admits no materially different implementation of
195	the predicate itself; it is output-forced, not copied.
196	
197	Decisively, the entire non-observable apparatus that surrounds those gates in the
198	source is ABSENT on the clean side: the product weight formula, the greedy
199	max-weight/decrement iteration, the soft-limit + always-abbrev-weight cutoff
200	(constants 10/30), the `relativeOccs` legend special-case, and the one-level
201	`getSubTerms`. Where the clean side had latitude it chose *differently* from the
202	source — full-recursive enumeration vs one-level, ascending-length ordering vs
203	greedy decreasing-weight, fully-expanded length vs abbrev-applied length, a
204	whole-system occurrence universe vs `grNodes`. These divergences are affirmative
205	evidence of derivation from observation rather than from the source.
206	
207	No shared internal names (clean `select`/`select_with`/`render_len`/`is_tuple`/
208	`for_each_subterm`/`size` vs HS `judgeTerm`/`computeAbbreviations`/`termWeight`/
209	`relativeOccs`/`getSubTerms`/`getFactTerms`/`aoAbbrevsSoftLimit`). No echoed
210	comments — the clean comments cite the clean room's own corpus/probe evidence,
211	whereas the HS comments describe the weight design and its `cleandot.py` origin.

213	### VIOLATIONS (Round 2)
214	None. No finding survives filtration as non-observable expression. No redo
215	instructions. VERDICT: PASS.

" 184 <> "\nWARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!

" 187 unless (null report) $ putStrLn $ renderDoc $ ppInteractive report path 188 let theoryInfo t = TheoryInfo idx t time Nothing True (Local path) autoProver wfErrors 189 pure $ Just (idx, either (Trace . theoryInfo) (Diff . theoryInfo) thy) 190 where 191 reportFailure err inFile = Pretty.vcat $ Pretty.text <$> 192 [ replicate 78 '-' 193 , "Unable to load theory file `" ++ inFile ++ "'" 194 , replicate 78 '-' 195 , "" 196 , show err 197 , replicate 78 '-' 198 , "" ] 199 200 ppInteractive report inFile = Pretty.vcat 201 [ Pretty.text $ replicate 78 '-' 202 , Pretty.text $ "Theory file '" ++ inFile ++ "'" 203 , Pretty.text $ replicate 78 '-' 204 , Pretty.text "" 205 , Pretty.text "WARNING: ignoring the following wellformedness errors" 206 , Pretty.text "" 207 , prettyWfErrorReport report 208 , Pretty.text $ replicate 78 '-' 209 , Pretty.text "" ] 210

7	--
8	-- Portability : GHC only
9	--
10	-- Representation of a graph as a collection of nodes, edges and clusters that can be used for rendering a System.
11	module Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.GraphRepr (
12	      GraphRepr(..)
13	    , grNodes
14	    , grClusters
15	    , grEdges
16	    , Node(..)
17	    , nNodeType
18	    , nNodeId
19	    , NodeType(..)
20	    , Edge(..)
21	    , Cluster(..)
22	    , cName
23	    , cNodes
24	    , cEdges
25	    , toEdgeList
26	    , extractRole
27	    , getNodeRole
28	    , getNodeName
29	    , groupNodesByRole
30	    , addClusterByRole
31	    , addIntelligentClusterUsingSimilarNames
32	    , extractBaseName
33	    , getRuleNameByNode
34	  ) where
35	
36	import           Extension.Data.Label
37	import qualified Theory.Constraint.System as Sys
38	import qualified Theory.Model             as M
39	import qualified Theory                   as Th
40	import qualified Data.Map                 as Map
41	
42	import qualified Data.Set                 as S
43	
44	import Data.Char (isDigit)
45	import Data.List.Split (splitOn)
46	import Data.List (find, intercalate)
47	import Data.Maybe
48	
49	-- | All nodes are identified by their NodeId.
50	-- Then we have different types of nodes depending on what data of the System they use.
51	data Node = Node {
52	    _nNodeId    :: M.NodeId,
53	    _nNodeType  :: NodeType
54	  }
55	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
56	
57	-- | Different types of graph nodes.
58	data NodeType =
59	    SystemNode Th.RuleACInst                    -- ^ Nodes from rule instances
60	  | UnsolvedActionNode [Th.LNFact]             -- ^ Nodes from unsolved adversary actions.
61	  | LastActionAtom                             -- ^ Nodes that are only used for induction.
62	  | MissingNode (Either Th.ConcIdx Th.PremIdx) -- ^ Nodes referenced by edges which don't exist elsewhere.
63	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
64	
65	
66	-- | Different types of graph edges.
67	data Edge =
68	    SystemEdge (Sys.NodeConc, Sys.NodePrem)    -- ^ Edges that transport facts from premises to conclusions between rules.
69	  | LessEdge Th.LessAtom                       -- ^ Edges that represent a temporal-before relationship.
70	  | UnsolvedChain (Sys.NodeConc, Sys.NodePrem) -- ^ Edges that are part of an unsolved chain between premises and conclusions.
71	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
72	
73	-- | A cluster contains nodes, edges, and a name, which is the common prefix of the contained nodes.
74	data Cluster = Cluster {
75	    _cName  :: String
76	  , _cNodes :: [Node]
77	  , _cEdges :: [Edge]
78	  }
79	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
80	
81	-- | A graph consists of nodes, edges and clusters which are only one level deep to represent a collection of derivation rules with the same prefix.
82	data GraphRepr = GraphRepr {
83	    _grClusters :: [Cluster]
84	  , _grNodes    :: [Node]
85	  , _grEdges    :: [Edge]
86	  }
87	  deriving ( Eq, Ord, Show )
88	
89	$(mkLabels [''GraphRepr, ''Node, ''Cluster])
90	
91	-- | Conversion function to a list of edges as used by Data.Graph.
92	toEdgeList :: GraphRepr -> [(Node, M.NodeId, [M.NodeId])]
93	toEdgeList repr =
94	  let allNodes = get grNodes repr ++ concatMap (get cNodes) (get grClusters repr)
95	      allEdges = get grEdges repr ++ concatMap (get cEdges) (get grClusters repr) in
96	  map (\node -> (node, get nNodeId node, findSinkIndices allEdges node)) allNodes
97	  where
98	    -- | For each node, find all connected nodes using allEdges and return their NodeId's.
99	    findSinkIndices :: [Edge] -> Node -> [M.NodeId]
100	    findSinkIndices allEdges node =
101	      let srcId = get nNodeId node in
102	      mapMaybe (findEdgeTarget srcId) allEdges
103	
104	    -- | For a given source node id and an edge, check if the edge belongs to the node and return the target node id.
105	    findEdgeTarget :: M.NodeId -> Edge -> Maybe M.NodeId
106	    findEdgeTarget srcId (SystemEdge ((srcId', _), (tgtId, _)))    | srcId == srcId' = Just tgtId
107	    findEdgeTarget srcId (LessEdge (Th.LessAtom srcId' tgtId _))   | srcId == srcId' = Just tgtId
108	    findEdgeTarget srcId (UnsolvedChain ((srcId', _), (tgtId, _))) | srcId == srcId' = Just tgtId
109	    findEdgeTarget _     _                                                           = Nothing
110	
111	
112	----------------------------------------------------
113	-- Clusturing
114	----------------------------------------------------
115	
116	-- Function to add clusters to a GraphRepr
117	addCluster :: GraphRepr -> Map.Map String [Node] -> String -> GraphRepr
118	addCluster repr nodesByGroup nameSuffix =
119	    let edges = get grEdges repr
120	        createSubClusters name nodes =
121	            let connectedComponents = findConnectedComponents nodes (filterEdgesForCluster nodes edges)
122	            in zipWith (\i component -> createCluster (name ++ nameSuffix ++ show (i :: Integer)) component (filterEdgesForCluster component edges)) [1..] connectedComponents
123	        subClusters = concatMap (uncurry createSubClusters) (Map.toList nodesByGroup)
124	        clusterEdges = concatMap (get cEdges) subClusters
125	        clusteredNodes = concatMap (get cNodes) subClusters
126	        remainingEdges = filter (`notElem` clusterEdges) edges
127	        remainingNodes = filter (`notElem` clusteredNodes) (get grNodes repr)
128	    in set grClusters subClusters $
129	       set grEdges remainingEdges $
130	       set grNodes remainingNodes repr
131	
132	----------------------------------------------------
133	-- Clusturing by role name
134	----------------------------------------------------
135	
136	extractRole :: Th.RuleACInst -> Maybe String
137	extractRole ru = Th.role (Th.ruleAttributes ru)
138	
139	groupNodesByRole :: [Node] -> Map.Map String [Node]
140	groupNodesByRole = foldr groupByRole Map.empty
141	  where
142	    groupByRole node acc = case getNodeRole node of
143	      Just role     -> Map.insertWith (++) role [node] acc
144	      Nothing        -> acc
145	
146	
147	getNodeName :: Node -> String
148	getNodeName node = "node" ++ show (get nNodeId node)
149	
150	getNodeRole :: Node -> Maybe String
151	getNodeRole node = case get nNodeType node of
152	  SystemNode ru -> extractRole ru
153	  _             -> Nothing
154	
155	
156	-- Function to create a cluster from an role's nodes and relevant edges
157	createCluster :: String -> [Node] -> [Edge] -> Cluster
158	createCluster = Cluster
159	
160	-- Filters edges to include only those relevant for the nodes of a cluster
161	filterEdgesForCluster :: [Node] -> [Edge] -> [Edge]
162	filterEdgesForCluster nodes edges =
163	    let nodeIds = S.fromList (map (get nNodeId) nodes)
164	    in filter (\edge -> case edge of
165	                            SystemEdge ((srcNode, _), (tgtNode, _)) -> srcNode `S.member` nodeIds && tgtNode `S.member` nodeIds
166	                            UnsolvedChain ((srcNode, _), (tgtNode, _)) -> srcNode `S.member` nodeIds && tgtNode `S.member` nodeIds
167	                            LessEdge (Th.LessAtom srcNode tgtNode _) -> srcNode `S.member` nodeIds && tgtNode `S.member` nodeIds) edges
168	
169	-- Function to find the connected components within a cluster
170	findConnectedComponents :: [Node] -> [Edge] -> [[Node]]
171	findConnectedComponents nodes edges = go nodes []
172	  where
173	    -- Recursive function to find all nodes connected from a given node
174	    expandCluster :: Node -> S.Set Th.NodeId -> [Node] -> [Edge] -> S.Set Th.NodeId
175	    expandCluster node visited allNodes allEdges =
176	      let nodeId = get nNodeId node
177	          connectedNodes = [ tgt | SystemEdge ((src, _), (tgt, _)) <- allEdges, src == nodeId, tgt `S.notMember` visited ] ++
178	                           [ src | SystemEdge ((src, _), (tgt, _)) <- allEdges, tgt == nodeId, src `S.notMember` visited ]
179	          newVisited = S.insert nodeId visited
180	      in foldr (\nid acc -> if nid `S.member` visited then acc else expandCluster (findNodeById nid allNodes) newVisited allNodes allEdges `S.union` acc) (S.singleton nodeId) connectedNodes
181	
182	    findNodeById :: Th.NodeId -> [Node] -> Node
183	    findNodeById nodeId allNodes = head $ filter (\n -> get nNodeId n == nodeId) allNodes
184	
185	    -- Main function to find all connected components
186	    go :: [Node] -> [[Node]] -> [[Node]]
187	    go [] components = components
188	    go (n:ns) components =
189	      let componentIds = S.toList $ expandCluster n S.empty (n:ns) edges
190	          component = filter (\node -> get nNodeId node `elem` componentIds) (n:ns)
191	          remainingNodes = filter (`notElem` component) ns
192	      in go remainingNodes (component : components)
193	
194	
195	-- Function to add sub-clusters by role
196	addClusterByRole :: GraphRepr -> GraphRepr
197	addClusterByRole repr =
198	    let nodesByRole = groupNodesByRole (get grNodes repr)
199	    in addCluster repr nodesByRole "_Session_"
200	
201	
202	
203	----------------------------------------------------
204	-- Clustering based on the name of the rules.
205	----------------------------------------------------
206	
207	-- Function to get the rule name from a node
208	getRuleNameByNode :: Node -> Maybe String
209	getRuleNameByNode node =
210	    case _nNodeType node of
211	        SystemNode ru -> case Th.ruleName ru of
212	                           Th.ProtoInfo _ -> Just (Th.showRuleCaseName ru)
213	                           _ -> Nothing
214	        _ -> Nothing
215	
216	-- Function to extract the base name based on underscores
217	extractBaseName :: String -> Maybe String
218	extractBaseName name =
219	    let parts = splitOn "_" name
220	        lastPart = last parts
221	        isNumber = all isDigit lastPart
222	        baseName = if isNumber && length parts > 1
223	                   then Just (intercalate "_" (init parts))
224	                   else Nothing
225	    in baseName
226	
227	-- Function to group nodes by similar rule names
228	groupBySimilarName :: [Node] -> Map.Map String [Node]
229	groupBySimilarName nodes =
230	    let result = foldr (\node acc ->
231	                    case getRuleNameByNode node >>= extractBaseName of
232	                        Just baseName -> Map.insertWith (++) baseName [node] acc
233	                        Nothing       -> acc
234	                ) Map.empty nodes
235	    in result
236	
237	-- Function to add intelligent clusters using similar rule names
238	addIntelligentClusterUsingSimilarNames :: GraphRepr -> GraphRepr
239	addIntelligentClusterUsingSimilarNames repr =
240	    let nodesBySimilarName = groupBySimilarName (get grNodes repr)
241	    in addCluster repr nodesBySimilarName "_Session_"

102	-- | Wrap a computation that generates dot components (SeDot) in order cache that component in the DotState and be able to retrieve its NodeId.
103	cacheState :: Ord k

165	-- | This function creates a cluster for a specific role with a list of nodes.
166	-- It takes an role name and a 'SeDot' action, then executing the given SeDot action within
167	--a temporary state, updating the current state, and adding the resulting subgraph elements to
168	--the global state.
169	
170	-- Note: To be compatible with the 'dotNodeCompact' function which returns a 'SeDot' action,
171	-- we needed a function that accepts and returns a 'SeDot' action. This is why we didn't use
172	-- the 'cluster' function from 'Text.Dot'. The only feasible approach was either this or
173	-- fragmenting the 'dotNodeCompact' function. Thus, we extracted what we needed and removed
174	-- the unnecessary parts.
175	roleCluster :: String -> SeDot a -> SeDot ()
176	roleCluster roleName dot = do
177	  uq <- liftDot D.nextId
178	  let cid = D.createClusterNodeId roleName
179	  env <- ask
180	  currentState <- State.get
181	  let clusterState = D.DotGenState { D._dgsId = uq, D._dgsElements = [] }
182	  ((_, newState), finalDotState) <- lift . lift . lift $ runStateT (runStateT (runReaderT dot env) currentState) clusterState
183	  State.put newState
184	  _ <- liftDot $ D.setId $ D._dgsId finalDotState
185	  liftDot $ D.addElements [D.createSubGraph (Just cid) (D._dgsElements finalDotState)]
186	
187	
188	-- | Compute a color map for nodes labelled with a proof rule info of one of
189	-- the given rules.
190	nodeColorMap :: [RuleACInst] -> NodeColorMap
191	nodeColorMap rules =
192	    M.fromList $
193	      [ (get rInfo ru, getColorForRule (ruleAttributes ru) gIdx mIdx)
194	      | (gIdx, grp) <- groups, (mIdx, ru) <- zip [0..] grp ]
195	  where
196	    groupIdx ru
197	      | isDestrRule ru                   = 0
198	      | isConstrRule ru                  = 2
199	      | isFreshRule ru || isISendRule ru = 3
200	      | otherwise                        = 1
201	
202	    -- groups of rules labeled with their index in the group
203	    groups = [ (gIdx, [ ru | ru <- rules, gIdx == groupIdx ru])
204	             | gIdx <- [0..3]
205	             ]
206	
207	    -- color for each member of a group
208	    colors = M.fromList $ lightColorGroups intruderHue (map (length . snd) groups)
209	    getColor idx = fromMaybe (HSV 0 1 1) $ M.lookup idx colors
210	
211	    -- Function to get color for a given rule
212	    getColorForRule attrs gIdx mIdx = fromMaybe defaultColor (ruleColor attrs)
213	        where
214	            defaultColor =  hsvToRGB $ getColor (gIdx, mIdx)
215	
216	    -- The hue of the intruder rules
217	    intruderHue :: Rational
218	    intruderHue = 18 % 360
219	
220	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
221	-- Record based dotting
222	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
223	
224	-- | Render an LNFact using the abbreviations given by the graph.
225	renderLNFact :: Document d => LNFact -> SeDot d
226	renderLNFact fact = do
227	  (graph, _, _) <- ask
228	  let abbreviate = get ((L..) goAbbreviate gOptions) graph
229	      abbrevs = get gAbbreviations graph
230	      replacedFact = applyAbbreviationsFact (lookupAbbreviation abbrevs) fact
231	  if abbreviate
232	    then return $ prettyLNFact replacedFact
233	    else return $ prettyLNFact fact
234	
235	-- | Dot a node in record based (compact) format.
236	dotNodeCompact :: Node -> Maybe String -> SeDot ()
237	dotNodeCompact node manualNodeColor = do
238	  let v = get nNodeId node
239	  (graph, colorMap, dotOptions) <- ask
240	  case get nNodeType node of
241	    SystemNode ru -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
242	      let outgoingEdge = hasOutgoingEdge graph v
243	      let role = fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)
244	
245	      let rInfoVal = get rInfo ru
246	
247	      -- TODO this is pretty ugly. Shorten using standard functions
248	      let ruleColor' = case rInfoVal of
249	                        ProtoInfo protoRule ->
250	                          case ruleColor (_praciAttributes protoRule) of
251	                            Just rgb -> Just (rgbToHex rgb)
252	                            _ -> Nothing
253	                        _ -> Nothing
254	
255	      let color = M.lookup rInfoVal colorMap
256	          nodeColor = fromMaybe (maybe "white" rgbToHex color) (ruleColor' <|> manualNodeColor)
257	          attrs = [("fillcolor", nodeColor), ("style", "filled")
258	                  , ("fontcolor", if colorUsesWhiteFont color then "white" else "black")
259	                  , ("role", role)]
260	
261	      ids <- mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
262	      let prems = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Left i),  nid) <- ids ]
263	          concs = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Right i), nid) <- ids ]
264	      modM dsPrems $ M.union $ M.fromList prems
265	      modM dsConcs $ M.union $ M.fromList concs
266	      return $ fromJust $ lookup Nothing ids
267	    UnsolvedActionNode facts -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
268	      lblPre <- (fsep <$> punctuate comma <$> mapM renderLNFact facts)
269	      let lbl = lblPre <-> opAction <-> text (show v)
270	      let attrs | any isKUFact facts = [("color","gray")]
271	                | otherwise          = [("color","darkblue")]
272	      mkSimpleNode (render lbl) attrs
273	    LastActionAtom -> cacheState dsNodes v $ mkSimpleNode (show v) []
274	    MissingNode (Left conc) -> cacheState dsConcs (v, conc) $ dotConcC (v, conc)
275	    MissingNode (Right prem) -> cacheState dsPrems (v, prem) $ dotPremC (v, prem)
276	  where
277	    hasOutgoingEdge graph v =
278	      let repr = get gRepr graph
279	      in or [ v == v' | SystemEdge ((v', _), _) <- get grEdges repr ]
280	    missingNode shape label = liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", render label),("shape",shape)]
281	    dotPremC prem = missingNode "invtrapezium" $ prettyNodePrem prem
282	    dotConcC conc = missingNode "trapezium" $ prettyNodeConc conc
283	
284	    --True if there's a colour, and it's 'darker' than 0.5 in apparent luminosity
285	    --This assumes a linear colourspace, which is what graphviz seems to use
286	    colorUsesWhiteFont (Just (RGB r g b)) = (0.2126*r + 0.7152*g + 0.0722*b) < 0.5
287	    colorUsesWhiteFont _                  = False
288	
289	    mkSimpleNode lbl attrs =
290	        liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", lbl),("shape","ellipse")] ++ attrs
291	
292	    mkNode  :: NodeId -> RuleACInst -> [(String, String)] -> Bool -> DotOptions
293	      -> SeDot [(Maybe (Either PremIdx ConcIdx), D.NodeId)]
294	    mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
295	      -- single node, share node-id for all premises and conclusions
296	      | get doNodeStyle dotOptions == CompactBoringNodes &&
297	        (isIntruderRule ru || isFreshRule ru) = do
298	            ps <- psM
299	            as <- asM
300	            cs <- csM
301	            let lbl | outgoingEdge = show v ++ " : " ++ showDotRuleCaseName ru
302	                    | otherwise       = concatMap snd as
303	            nid <- mkSimpleNode lbl []
304	            return [ (key, nid) | (key, _) <- ps ++ as ++ cs ]
305	      -- full record syntax
306	      | otherwise = do
307	            ps <- psM
308	            as <- asM
309	            cs <- csM
310	            fmap snd $ liftDot $ (`D.record` attrs) $
311	              D.vcat $ map D.hcat $ map (map (uncurry D.portField)) $
312	              filter (not . null) [ps, as, cs]
313	      where
314	        psM = do
315	          row <- mapM (\(i, p) -> do
316	            lbl <- renderLNFact p
317	            return (Just (Left i), lbl)
318	            ) $ enumPrems ru
319	          return $ renderRow row
320	        asM = do
321	          ruleLabel <- ruleLabelM
322	          return $ renderRow [ (Nothing, ruleLabel ) ]
323	        csM = do
324	          row <- mapM (\(i, c) -> do
325	            lbl <- renderLNFact c
326	            return (Just (Right i), lbl)
327	            ) $ enumConcs ru
328	          return $ renderRow row
329	
330	        ruleLabelM = do
331	          showAutoSource <- asks (get ((L..) goShowAutoSource gOptions) . fst3)
332	          lbl <- if showAutoSource
333	                      then mapM renderLNFact $ filter isAutoSource $ filter isNotDiffAnnotation $ get rActs ru
334	                      else mapM renderLNFact $ filter isNotDiffAnnotation $ get rActs ru
335	          return $
336	            prettyNodeId v <-> colon
337	            <-> text (showDotRuleCaseName ru)
338	            <> (if null lbl then mempty else brackets (vcat $ punctuate comma lbl))
339	
340	
341	        isNotDiffAnnotation fa = (fa /= (Fact (ProtoFact Linear ("Diff" ++ getRuleNameDiff ru) 0) S.empty []))
342	
343	        -- check if a fact is from auto-source
344	        isAutoSource ::  LNFact -> Bool
345	        isAutoSource (Fact tag _ _) =not $ hasAutoLabel (showFactTag $ tag)
346	
347	        -- check if a fact has the label of auto-source
348	        hasAutoLabel :: String -> Bool
349	        hasAutoLabel f
350	            | "AUTO_IN_TERM_" `isPrefixOf` f  = True
351	            | "AUTO_IN_FACT_" `isPrefixOf` f  = True
352	            | "AUTO_OUT_TERM_" `isPrefixOf` f = True
353	            | "AUTO_OUT_FACT_" `isPrefixOf` f = True
354	            | otherwise = False
355	
356	
357	        renderRow annDocs =
358	          zipWith (\(ann, _) lbl -> (ann, lbl)) annDocs $
359	            -- magic factor 1.3 compensates for space gained due to
360	            -- non-propertional font
361	            renderBalanced 100 (max 30 . round . (* 1.3)) (map snd annDocs)
362	
363	        renderBalanced :: Double           -- ^ Total available width
364	                       -> (Double -> Int)  -- ^ Convert available space to actual line-width.
365	                       -> [Doc]            -- ^ Initial documents
366	                       -> [String]         -- ^ Rendered documents
367	        renderBalanced _          _    []   = []
368	        renderBalanced totalWidth conv docs =
369	            zipWith (\w d -> widthRender (conv (ratio * w)) d) usedWidths docs
370	          where
371	            oneLineRender  = renderStyle (defaultStyle { mode = OneLineMode })
372	            widthRender w  = scaleIndent . renderStyle (defaultStyle { lineLength = w })
373	            usedWidths     = map (fromIntegral . length . oneLineRender) docs
374	            ratio          = totalWidth / sum usedWidths
375	            scaleIndent line = case span isSpace line of
376	              (spaces, rest) ->
377	                  -- spaces are not wide-enough by default => scale them up
378	                  let n = (1.5::Double) * fromIntegral (length spaces)
379	                  in  replicate (round n) ' ' ++ rest
380	
381	
382	-- | Dot a normal edge.
383	dotEdge :: Edge -> SeDot ()
384	dotEdge edge =
385	  case edge of
386	    SystemEdge (src, tgt) -> do
387	      (graph, _, _) <- ask
388	      let check p = maybe False p (resolveNodePremFact tgt graph) ||
389	                    maybe False p (resolveNodeConcFact src graph)
390	          attrs | check isProtoFact =
391	                    [("style","bold"),("weight","10.0")] ++
392	                    (guard (check isPersistentFact) >> [("color","gray50")])
393	                | check isKFact     = [("color","orangered2")]
394	                | otherwise         = [("color","gray30")]
395	      dotGenEdge attrs src tgt
396	    UnsolvedChain (src, tgt) ->
397	      dotGenEdge [("style","dotted"),("color","green")] src tgt
398	    LessEdge _ -> error "LessEdges are handled by dotLessEdge"
399	  where
400	    dotGenEdge style src tgt = do
401	      srcId <- getState dsConcs src ("Source node of edge not found: " ++ show src)
402	      tgtId <- getState dsPrems tgt ("Target node of edge not found: " ++ show tgt)
403	      liftDot $ D.edge srcId tgtId style
404	
405	-- | Dot a less edge, which needs to be transformed first to contain the correct color.
406	dotLessEdge :: (NodeId, NodeId, String) -> SeDot ()
407	dotLessEdge (src, tgt, color) = do
408	  srcId <- getState dsNodes src ("Source node of less edge not found: " ++ show src)
409	  tgtId <- getState dsNodes tgt ("Target node of less edge not found: " ++ show src)
410	  liftDot $ D.edge srcId tgtId [("color",color),("style","dashed")]
411	
412	-- | Dot a legend listing all abbreviations by adding a sink node with a suitable HTML table label.
413	generateLegend :: SeDot ()
414	generateLegend = do
415	  (graph, _, dotOptions) <- ask
416	  let abbrevs = get gAbbreviations graph
417	  -- Skip generating anything if no abbreviations exist.
418	  unless (null abbrevs) $ do
419	    nLegend <- liftDot $ D.scope (do
420	      D.attribute ("rank", "sink")
421	      let sortedAbbrevs = topoSortAbbrevs $ zip [0..] $
422	            sortOn (Data.Ord.Down . render . Sys.prettyLNTerm . fst) $ M.elems abbrevs
423	          labelColor = get doAbbrevColor dotOptions
424	          htmlLabel = D.htmlLabel $ abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor
425	      D.node [("shape", "plain"), htmlLabel])
426	    -- We add invisible edges from all sink nodes of the graph to the legend node to place it somewhere in the middle of the bottom row.
427	    -- We only add edges from the sink nodes because edges from earlier nodes will be routed avoid later nodes (even if they are invisible) and create constraints that lead to excessive whitespace on the edges of the graph.
428	    let sinks = getGraphSinks graph
429	    dotIds <- getM dsNodes
430	    mapM_ (\nsink ->
431	      case M.lookup (get nNodeId nsink) dotIds of
432	      Nothing -> pure ()
433	      Just nid -> liftDot $ D.edge nid nLegend [("style", "invis")]) sinks
434	  where
435	    -- | Render all abbreviations as a table using graphviz' HTML notation.
436	    abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor =
437	      let tableAttributes = [Border 1, CellBorder 0, CellSpacing 3, CellPadding 1] in
438	        Table $ HTable Nothing tableAttributes $ map (renderLine labelColor) sortedAbbrevs
439	
440	    -- | Render an abbreviation to a table row in the legend using graphviz' HTML notation.
441	    renderLine :: D.Color -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> Row
442	    renderLine labelColor (abbrevName, recursiveExpansion) =
443	      let cellAttributes = [Align HLeft, VAlign HTop]
444	          font txt = D.Text [Font [Color labelColor] txt]
445	          name = LabelCell cellAttributes (font [Str $ T.pack $ render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm abbrevName])
446	          eq = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text [Str $ "="])
447	          expansion = render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm recursiveExpansion
448	          -- The expansions can get pretty big, i.e. span multiple lines. To handle linebreaks we replace them with HTML <br> tags.
449	          expansionBR = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text $ expansion `joinLinesWith` Newline [Align HLeft]) in
450	      Cells [name, eq, expansionBR]
451	
452	    -- | Replace newlines in `s` with the given separator.
453	    joinLinesWith :: String -> TextItem -> D.Text
454	    joinLinesWith s sep = intersperse sep $ map (Str . T.pack) $ lines s
455	
456	    -- | Do a topological sort of abbreviations to ensure that we print abbreviations in the order in which they are defined.
457	    -- We define a partial order where for two abbreviations A & B, it holds A < B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
458	    -- To extend the partial order to all abbreviations we do a topological sort on the graph where the nodes are abbreviations and edges are based on the partial order.
459	    topoSortAbbrevs :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> [(AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion)]
460	    topoSortAbbrevs keyedElems =
461	      let edgeList = map (\(key1, node) ->
462	                            let outlist = findLegendEdges keyedElems node in
463	                            (node, key1, outlist)) keyedElems
464	          (graph, vf, _) = G.graphFromEdges edgeList
465	          vertices = G.topSort graph in
466	      map (\v -> fst3 $ vf v) vertices
467	
468	    -- | We create an edge A -> B between two abbreviations A & B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
469	    findLegendEdges :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> [Int]
470	    findLegendEdges keyedElems (abbrevName1, _) =
471	      mapMaybe (\(key2, (_, recursiveExpansion2)) ->
472	                  if isProperSubterm abbrevName1 recursiveExpansion2
473	                  then Just key2
474	                  else Nothing) keyedElems
475	
476	
477	
478	
479	-- | Dot a sequent in compact form (one record per rule), if there is anything
480	-- to draw.
481	dotSystemCompact :: GraphOptions -> DotOptions -> System -> D.Dot ()
482	dotSystemCompact graphOptions dotOptions se =
483	    let graph = systemToGraph se graphOptions
484	        -- a.d. TODO make nodeColorMap filter systemnodes from graph instead of accessing System directly.
485	        colorMap = nodeColorMap (M.elems $ get sNodes se)
486	        dot = dotGraphCompact dotOptions colorMap graph in
487	    dot
488	
489	-- | Dot a graph in compact form (one record per rule).
490	dotGraphCompact :: DotOptions -> NodeColorMap -> Graph -> D.Dot ()
491	dotGraphCompact dotOptions colorMap graph =
492	    (`evalStateT` DotState M.empty M.empty M.empty M.empty) $
493	        (`runReaderT` (graph, colorMap, dotOptions)) $ do
494	
495	            -- Get the graph representation details
496	            let repr = get gRepr graph
497	                clusters = get grClusters repr
498	                edges = get grEdges repr
499	                nodes = get grNodes repr
500	                (lessEdges, restEdges) = mergeLessEdges edges
501	                abbreviate = get ((L..) goAbbreviate gOptions) graph
502	
503	            if null $ get grClusters repr then liftDot setDefaultAttributes else liftDot setDefaultAttributesIfCluster
504	
505	            -- Process the nodes, clusters, and edges
506	            mapM_ (\node -> dotNodeCompact node Nothing) nodes
507	            mapM_ dotCluster clusters
508	            mapM_ dotEdge restEdges
509	            mapM_ dotLessEdge lessEdges
510	            dotClustersEdges clusters
511	
512	            -- Generate legend if abbreviate is set
513	            when abbreviate generateLegend
514	
515	
516	-- Function to dot edges from multiple clusters
517	dotClustersEdges :: [Cluster] -> SeDot ()
518	dotClustersEdges clusters = do
519	    let allEdges = concatMap (get cEdges) clusters
520	        (lessEdges, restEdges) = mergeLessEdges allEdges
521	    mapM_ dotEdge restEdges
522	    mapM_ dotLessEdge lessEdges
523	
524	
525	-- Simple hash function to amplify differences between names
526	simpleHash :: String -> Int
527	simpleHash = foldl (\ acc c -> acc * 31 + ord c) 7
528	
529	-- Function to generate a value based on the role name
530	generateValue :: String -> Double
531	generateValue s = fromIntegral (simpleHash s `mod` 360) / 360.0
532	
533	-- Function to generate a color based on the role name with more aesthetic colors
534	roleColor :: String -> String
535	roleColor name = printf "#%02X%02X%02X%02X" r g b alpha
536	  where
537	    v = generateValue name
538	    -- Adjust saturation and value to get more pleasant colors
539	    RGB rf gf bf = hsvToRGB (HSV (v * 360) 0.75 0.85) :: RGB Double  -- Higher saturation and brightness
540	    r = floor (rf * 255) :: Int
541	    g = floor (gf * 255) :: Int
542	    b = floor (bf * 255) :: Int
543	    alpha :: Int
544	    alpha = floor (255 * (0.3 :: Double))
545	
546	-- Function to dot clusters
547	dotCluster :: Cluster -> SeDot ()
548	dotCluster (Cluster name nodes _) = do
549	    let baseName = fromMaybe "Undefined" (extractBaseName name)
550	    let color = roleColor baseName
551	    roleCluster name $ do
552	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("nodesep", "0.6")
553	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("ranksep", "0.6")
554	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("label", name)
555	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("style", "filled") -- Set style to filled to apply fillcolor
556	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("color", color)
557	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("penwidth", "2")
558	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("fillcolor", color) -- Use roleColor to set the fillcolor
559	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("overlap", "false")
560	        liftDot $ D.attribute ("sep", "4")
561	
562	        mapM_ (\node -> dotNodeCompact node (Just color)) nodes
563	
564	-- | Compute proper colors for all less-edges.
565	-- Computing the colors requires all less-edges of the graph, so we cannot do it per edge.
566	-- This is why we split up the edges here and use two different functions to convert them to a dot.
567	mergeLessEdges :: [Edge] -> ([(NodeId, NodeId, String)], [Edge])
568	mergeLessEdges edges = (merged, rest)
569	  where
570	    rest = filter (not . isLessEdge) edges
571	    merged =
572	      let lessEdges = mapMaybe extractLessEdge edges in
573	      map getAllRToC $ eqClasses (\(LessAtom x y _) -> (x, y)) lessEdges
574	
575	    isLessEdge :: Edge -> Bool
576	    isLessEdge (LessEdge _) = True
577	    isLessEdge _ = False
578	
579	    extractLessEdge :: Edge -> Maybe LessAtom
580	    extractLessEdge (LessEdge e) = Just e
581	    extractLessEdge _ = Nothing
582	
583	    getAllRToC :: [LessAtom] -> (NodeId, NodeId, String)
584	    -- SAFETY: Output of eqClasses never contains the empty list.
585	    getAllRToC [] = error "empty list"
586	    getAllRToC xs@(x:_) =
587	      (get laSmaller x, get laLarger x,
588	        -- Sort order is reversed to put the "most important reason" first.
589	        allRtoColors (sortBy (comparing Data.Ord.Down) $ map (get laReason) xs))
590	
591	    allRtoColors :: [Reason] -> String
592	    allRtoColors reasons =
593	      let len = length reasons
594	          per = if len >1 then ";" ++ show ((1 :: Double)/fromIntegral len) else ""
595	          colors = map toColor reasons
596	          scaledColors = map (++per) colors
597	      in intercalate ":" scaledColors
598	
599	    toColor :: Reason -> String
600	    toColor r = case r of
601	         Adversary      -> "red"
602	         Formula        -> "black"
603	         Fresh          -> "blue3"
604	         InjectiveFacts -> "purple"
605	         NormalForm     -> "darkorange3"
606

1	{-# LANGUAGE TypeOperators   #-}
2	
3	-- |
4	-- Copyright   : (c) 2010, 2011 Simon Meier
5	-- License     : GPL v3 (see LICENSE)
6	--
7	-- Portability : GHC only
8	--
9	-- System simplification functions that were originally contained in the Dot module 
10	-- but which we now do on a System when generating an abstract graph.
11	module Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.Simplification (
12	      simplifySystem
13	    , compressSystem
14	  )
15	  where
16	
17	import qualified Data.Map                 as M
18	import           Data.Maybe
19	import qualified Data.Set                 as S
20	import           Data.List                (foldl')
21	import           Control.Basics
22	import           Extension.Data.Label
23	-- import           Theory.Constraint.System
24	import           Theory                   
25	import qualified Data.DAG.Simple          as Dag
26	import           Data.Monoid              (Any(..))
27	
28	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	-- Compressed versions of a sequent (originally from Dot module)
30	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	
32	-- | Drop 'Less' atoms entailed by the edges of the 'System'.
33	dropEntailedOrdConstraints :: System -> System
34	dropEntailedOrdConstraints se =
35	    modify sLessAtoms (S.filter (not . entailed)) se
36	  where
37	    edges               = rawEdgeRel se
38	    entailed (LessAtom from to _) = to `S.member` Dag.reachableSet [from] edges
39	
40	-- | Unsound compression of the sequent that drops fully connected learns and
41	-- knows nodes.
42	compressSystem :: System -> System
43	compressSystem se0 =
44	    foldl' (flip tryHideNodeId) se (frees (get sLessAtoms se, get sNodes se))
45	  where
46	    se = dropEntailedOrdConstraints se0
47	
48	-- | Simplify the system up to the sent level, 
49	-- | for level 3, we will simplify the lesses of system by transitive reduction
50	-- | for level 2, we will apply the transitive reduction but keep the 
51	-- | formula constraint 
52	-- | for level 1, there's no transitive reduction applied
53	simplifySystem :: Int -> System -> System
54	simplifySystem i sys
55	    | i==2 = transitiveReduction sys False
56	    | i==3 = transitiveReduction sys True
57	    | otherwise = sys
58	
59	-- | Simplify the system by transitive reduction (constraint of formula won't  
60	-- | be applied if totalRed is False) but not for a system which has a graph cyclic
61	transitiveReduction :: System -> Bool -> System
62	transitiveReduction sys totalRed=
63	    if Dag.cyclic oldLesses
64	        then sys
65	        else   modify sLessAtoms
66	            ( S.intersection ( S.fromList newLesses) ) sys
67	    where
68	        oldLessesWithR = S.toList $ get sLessAtoms sys
69	        oldLesses = rawLessRel sys
70	        newLesses = if totalRed
71	            then [ la | la@(LessAtom x y _) <- oldLessesWithR,
72	                            (x,y) `elem` Dag.transRed oldLesses ]
73	            else [ la | la@(LessAtom x y z) <- oldLessesWithR,
74	                            (x,y) `elem` Dag.transRed oldLesses || z == Formula || z == Adversary ]
75	
76	
77	-- | @hideTransferNode v se@ hides node @v@ in sequent @se@ if it is a
78	-- transfer node; i.e., a node annotated with a rule that is one of the
79	-- special intruder rules or a rule with with at most one premise and
80	-- at most one conclusion and both premises and conclusions have incoming
81	-- respectively outgoing edges.
82	--
83	-- The compression is chosen such that unly uninteresting nodes are that have
84	-- no open goal are suppressed.
85	tryHideNodeId :: NodeId -> System -> System
86	tryHideNodeId v se = fromMaybe se $ do
87	    guard $  (lvarSort v == LSortNode)
88	          && notOccursIn unsolvedChains
89	          && notOccursIn (get sFormulas)
90	    maybe hideAction hideRule (M.lookup v $ get sNodes se)
91	  where
92	    selectPart :: (System :-> S.Set a) -> (a -> Bool) -> [a]
93	    selectPart l p = filter p $ S.toList $ get l se
94	
95	    notOccursIn :: HasFrees a => (System -> a) -> Bool
96	    notOccursIn proj = not $ getAny $ foldFrees (Any . (v ==)) $ proj se
97	
98	    -- hide KU-actions deducing pairs, inverses, and simple terms
99	    hideAction = do
100	        guard $  not (null kuActions)
101	              && all eligibleTerm kuActions
102	              && all (\(LessAtom i j _) -> i /= j) lNews
103	              && notOccursIn standardActionAtoms
104	              && notOccursIn (get sLastAtom)
105	              && notOccursIn (get sEdges)
106	
107	        return $ modify sLessAtoms ( (`S.union` S.fromList lNews)
108	                                   . (`S.difference` S.fromList lIns)
109	                                   . (`S.difference` S.fromList lOuts)
110	                                   )
111	               $ modify sGoals (\m -> foldl' removeAction m kuActions)
112	               $ se
113	      where
114	        kuActions            = [ x | x@(i,_,_) <- kuActionAtoms se, i == v ]
115	        eligibleTerm (_,_,m) =
116	            isPair m || isInverse m || sortOfLNTerm m == LSortPub || sortOfLNTerm m == LSortNat
117	
118	        removeAction m (i, fa, _) = M.delete (ActionG i fa) m
119	
120	        lIns  = selectPart sLessAtoms ((v ==) . get laLarger)
121	        lOuts = selectPart sLessAtoms ((v ==) . get laSmaller)
122	        lNews = [ LessAtom i j r | (LessAtom i _ _) <- lIns, (LessAtom _ j r) <- lOuts ]
123	
124	    -- hide a rule, if it is not "too complicated"
125	    hideRule :: RuleACInst -> Maybe System
126	    hideRule ru = do
127	        guard $  eligibleRule
128	              && ( length eIns  == length (get rPrems ru) )
129	              && ( length eOuts == length (get rConcs ru) )
130	              && ( all (not . selfEdge) eNews             )
131	              && notOccursIn (get sLastAtom)
132	              && notOccursIn (get sLessAtoms)
133	              && notOccursIn (unsolvedActionAtoms)
134	
135	        return $ modify sEdges ( (`S.union` S.fromList eNews)
136	                               . (`S.difference` S.fromList eIns)
137	                               . (`S.difference` S.fromList eOuts)
138	                               )
139	               $ modify sNodes (M.delete v)
140	               $ se
141	      where
142	        eIns  = selectPart sEdges ((v ==) . nodePremNode . eTgt)
143	        eOuts = selectPart sEdges ((v ==) . nodeConcNode . eSrc)
144	        eNews = [ Edge cIn pOut | Edge cIn _ <- eIns, Edge _ pOut <- eOuts ]
145	
146	        selfEdge (Edge cIn pOut) = nodeConcNode cIn == nodePremNode pOut
147	
148	        eligibleRule =
149	             any ($ ru) [isISendRule, isIRecvRule, isCoerceRule, isFreshRule]
150	          || ( null (get rActs ru) &&
151	               all (\l -> length (get l ru) <= 1) [rPrems, rConcs]
152	             )

10	-- Abstraction over a System to represent certain components as a graph for rendering to a UI.
11	module Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.Graph (
12	      systemToGraph
13	    , SimplificationLevel(..)

33	import qualified Data.Map                 as M

42	import Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.Simplification (simplifySystem, compressSystem)
43	
44	-- | The level of graph simplification.
45	data SimplificationLevel = SL0 | SL1 | SL2 | SL3
46	    deriving( Eq, Ord, Read, Show )
47	
48	-- | Numberical representation of a 'SimplificationLevel'.
49	levelNum :: SimplificationLevel -> Int
50	levelNum SL0 = 0
51	levelNum SL1 = 1
52	levelNum SL2 = 2
53	levelNum SL3 = 3
54	
55	-- | Options for the graph generation.
56	data GraphOptions = GraphOptions
57	  { _goSimplificationLevel :: SimplificationLevel -- ^ The simplification level for simplifying the initial 'System'.
58	  , _goShowAutoSource      :: Bool                -- ^ Whether to show auto sources like "AUTO_xxx". a.d. TODO this maybe belongs in the DotOptions, not sure if auto source hiding is relevant for JSON.
59	  , _goClustering          :: Bool                -- ^ Whether to generate clusters of rules with common prefixes.
60	  , _goAbbreviate          :: Bool                -- ^ Whether to generate abbreviations.
61	  , _goCompress            :: Bool                -- ^ Whether to compress the initial 'System'.
62	  }
63	    deriving( Eq, Ord )
64	
65	-- | The default options for graph generation.
66	defaultGraphOptions :: GraphOptions
67	defaultGraphOptions = GraphOptions
68	  { _goSimplificationLevel = SL2
69	  , _goShowAutoSource = False
70	  , _goClustering = False
71	  , _goAbbreviate = True
72	  , _goCompress = True
73	  }
74	
75	-- | An abstract graph to derive visualiations of a 'System'.
76	data Graph = Graph
77	  { _gSystem        :: Sys.System    -- ^ The backing 'System' instance.
78	  , _gOptions       :: GraphOptions  -- ^ The options which influence graph generation.
79	  , _gRepr          :: GraphRepr     -- ^ The actual representation in terms of nodes, edges & clusters.
80	  , _gAbbreviations :: Abbreviations -- ^ The map of generated abbreviations.
81	  }
82	    deriving( Eq, Ord )
83	
84	$(mkLabels [''Graph, ''GraphOptions])
85	
86	-- | All facts associated to this node premise.
87	resolveNodePremFact :: Sys.NodePrem -> Graph -> Maybe Th.LNFact
88	resolveNodePremFact prem graph =
89	  let se = get gSystem graph in
90	  Sys.resolveNodePremFact prem se
91	
92	-- | The fact associated with this node conclusion, if there is one.
93	resolveNodeConcFact :: Sys.NodeConc -> Graph -> Maybe Th.LNFact
94	resolveNodeConcFact conc graph =
95	  let se = get gSystem graph in
96	  Sys.resolveNodeConcFact conc se
97	
98	-- | Get all nodes from a 'System' corresponding to rule instances.
99	systemNodes :: Sys.System -> [Node]
100	systemNodes se = map systemNode (M.toList $ get Sys.sNodes se)
101	  where
102	    systemNode (nid, ru) = Node nid (SystemNode ru)
103	
104	-- | Get all nodes from a 'System' corresponding to unsolved inputs by the adversary.
105	systemUnsolvedActionNodes :: Sys.System -> [Node]
106	systemUnsolvedActionNodes se = map unsolvedActionNode (collectBy $ Sys.unsolvedActionAtoms se)
107	  where
108	    unsolvedActionNode (nid, facts) = Node nid (UnsolvedActionNode facts)
109	
110	-- | Get all nodes from a 'System' corresponding to an induction node.
111	systemLastActionNode :: Sys.System -> [Node]
112	systemLastActionNode se = maybe [] (\nid -> [Node nid LastActionAtom]) (get Sys.sLastAtom se)
113	
114	-- | Get all nodes from a 'System' that are "missing", i.e. they are mentioned by an edge but don't exist elsewhere.
115	-- a.d. This assumes that there is no edge where both the source and target are missing. But that situation should never happen.
116	systemMissingNodes :: Sys.System -> [Node]
117	systemMissingNodes se = mapMaybe missingNode (S.toList $ get Sys.sEdges se)
118	  where
119	    missingNode (Sys.Edge (nid, idx) _) | nid `notElem` nodelist = Just $ Node nid (MissingNode (Left idx))
120	    missingNode (Sys.Edge _ (nid, idx)) | nid `notElem` nodelist = Just $ Node nid (MissingNode (Right idx))
121	    missingNode _ = Nothing
122	    nodelist = map fst $ M.toList $ get Sys.sNodes se
123	
124	-- | Get all edges from a 'System' corresponding to edges between rule instances.
125	systemEdges :: Sys.System -> [Edge]
126	systemEdges se =
127	  let edges = S.toList $ get Sys.sEdges se in
128	  map (\(Sys.Edge src tgt) -> SystemEdge (src, tgt)) edges
129	
130	-- | Computes a basic graph representation from a System
131	-- where nodes are
132	-- 1. the System rule instances
133	-- 2. unsolved actions by the attacker
134	-- 3. a possible last action (for induction)
135	-- 4. and any nodes that are required by edges but don't exist otherwise.
136	-- and edges are
137	-- 1. edges between rule instances
138	-- 2. edges implied by less-constraints between temporal variables
139	-- 3. and any unsolved chains.
140	computeBasicGraphRepr :: Sys.System -> GraphRepr
141	computeBasicGraphRepr se =
142	  let nodes = systemNodes se
143	        ++ systemUnsolvedActionNodes se
144	        ++ systemLastActionNode se
145	        ++ systemMissingNodes se
146	      edges =  systemEdges se
147	        ++ map LessEdge (S.toList $ get Sys.sLessAtoms se)
148	        ++ map UnsolvedChain (Sys.unsolvedChains se)
149	  in
150	    GraphRepr [] nodes edges
151	
152	-- | Compute clusters, nodes & edges from a Graph instance according to the Graph's options.
153	systemToGraph :: Sys.System -> GraphOptions -> Graph
154	systemToGraph se options =
155	  let -- We first do the existing simplification steps on a System that were defined in the Dot module originally.
156	      simplfiedSystem = simplifySystem (levelNum $ get goSimplificationLevel options) $
157	                          if get goCompress options then compressSystem se else se
158	      basicGraphRepr = computeBasicGraphRepr simplfiedSystem
159	      -- Iterate on the basicGraphRepr depending on what options are set to get the final repr
160	      repr = if get goClustering options
161	             then addIntelligentClusterUsingSimilarNames basicGraphRepr
162	             else addClusterByRole basicGraphRepr
163	      abbrevs = computeAbbreviations repr defaultAbbreviationOptions
164	  in
165	    Graph se options repr abbrevs
166	
167	-- | Get all sink nodes of a graph, i.e. those without outgoing edges.
168	getGraphSinks :: Graph -> [Node]
169	getGraphSinks graph =
170	  let repr = get gRepr graph
171	      edgeList = toEdgeList repr in
172	  map (\(node, _, _) -> node) $ filter (\(_, _, outlist) -> null outlist) edgeList
